data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and — when a task calls for it — work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy — so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- When your work completes or blocks a task/roadmap milestone, leave a concise task or roadmap comment with the decisive checks, changed files, and remaining caveats. If a task has linked `roadmap_refs`, keep your report/comment explicit about the status each linked roadmap item should have so Ground or the human can keep the roadmap consistent.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- Commit your work yourself as you finish coherent pieces — `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>` (see the horizon-commit skill). Small semantic commits (a lemma closed, a file completed) make the history and the dashboard's change view legible; don't leave one undifferentiated blob for the end-of-session sweep. Other runs may commit concurrently to the same ledger — that's fine; commit only the files you changed.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0008/sessions/0003-horizon-T5

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground recommendation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session; treat it as fresh guidance, not as a hard command if the live state contradicts it.
# Recommendation — run 0008 opening Ground (focus T5, `AJC.picrep` spine)

Suggestions for the first Horizon agent of this run. You self-scope; these are grounded in what I saw, not orders.

## Next

- **The FGA `picSharp` foundation is the single gate for the whole picrep/pic0av/jacobian spine, so it is the highest-value FBC-free target.** `picSharp` is still 7 `⟨sorry⟩` typed instances in `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean` — an opaque `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩ Nonempty` with NO defining property. Every downstream leaf (IdentityComponent's 6 sorries, `pic0av` `tangentSpaceIso`) inherits that taint, so nothing on the H¹ side can attach until this is rewired.

- **The substrate for the rewire now exists**: `PicSharp.relPresheaf` / `relFunctorial` / `toRelPresheaf` are real and axiom-clean in `Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` (see `I-0060`). The missing layer is `relEtSheaf` (does not exist yet — the étale-sheaf layer is still built on the *absolute* `presheaf`).

- **Decide the étale-topology route first, it is the one real blocker.** Mathlib v4.31 has no étale Grothendieck topology on schemes. Two options: (a) parametrize the sheafification topology `J`; (b) **Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5** — under a `k`-rational-section hypothesis `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so state representability against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis. Route (b) looks the more pragmatic path to a sorry-free rewire (the curve `C/k` in this problem has a rational point).

- **TRAP (do not do the naive rewire):** wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the *absolute* iso-class functor) makes `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement — absolute `Pic(C×T)` differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)` and is not representable. Any rewire MUST target the relative (and in general sheafified) functor. See memory `I-0061`.

- **Blueprint follows the same gate — do NOT repin piecemeal.** `I-0062` documents that `def:rel_pic_sharp` / `lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial` / `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf` carry `\leanok` but pin the absolute-Pic decls. The honest relative counterparts already exist (`subsec:relpic_relative_functor`, `def:rel_pic_sharp_relative` → `relPresheaf`). Repin the headline family to the relative decls **only as one coordinated Lean+blueprint step together with the `relEtSheaf`/rewire** — repinning def→relative while functorial/presheaf stay absolute just trades one inconsistency for another.

- **Do NOT re-probe the 6 IdentityComponent sorries or duplicate `AJC.pic0av`.** Verified blocked repeatedly: 5 Pic⁰ leaves are FGA-tainted (unblocked only by the rewire above) and the `GeometricallyIrreducible` conjunct needs EGA IV₂ 4.6.1-type input. See memory `I-0036`, `I-0052`.

## Watch-outs

- `SubProjects/Picard-IdentityComponent` mirror is **stale/behind AJC** (needs `IdentityComponent.lean` + `GeometricallyConnectedSection.lean` + blueprint re-sync, or retirement). Out of this Ground's write scope; flag before relying on it.
- The `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated-exponential split sequence (Kleiman Thm 5.11) is the *largest* remaining block after the rewire — the `tangentSpaceIso` numerator. Not this session's target, but it is what the rewire unblocks.
- Concurrent-build hazard is real (`I-0054`, `I-0063`): the RelPicFunctor/FGA cone is FBC-free, so it does not touch the `Cech*` chain — but still check `pgrep -f bin/lean` before any `lake build`.

# Recommended focus
Task: T5
Title: Extract & prove Pic⁰ IdentityComponent (FBC-independent picrep spine)
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/IdentityComponent*.lean

Advance the Picard-representability (picrep) spine WITHOUT depending on flat base change (FBC/02KH), so this runs fully in parallel with the live FBC run (T2) in AJC.

STEP 0 (gate — do this first, report before proceeding): Verify that the `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` import cone is genuinely FBC/cohomology-independent. Trace its transitive imports in MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge. If the cone pulls in the Čech/cohomology base-change engine (Cohomology/Cech*, the 02KH flat-base-change modules), STOP and report — do not extract; instead reduce scope to only the FBC-free declarations. If clean, continue.

STEP 1 (extraction — own build dir, MUST NOT build the AJC tree): Create a NEW standalone Lean subproject (e.g. SubProjects/Picard-IdentityComponent) with its own lakefile.toml (require mathlib rev v4.31.0, packagesDir ../../.lake-packages), lean-toolchain leanprover/lean4:v4.31.0, and its own .lake — mirroring the Albanese/GR-Quot-Closure extraction pattern. Copy in `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` and the minimal import cone needed to build it. Register it with `horizon project add` (enable vcs git_dir). The point of a separate subproject is build isolation: it must NOT touch or rebuild the AJC .lake that T2 is using.

STEP 2 (prove): Close the 9 structural sorries in IdentityComponent — Pic⁰ as the open-and-closed connected-component subgroup functor and the abelian-group-structure (Yoneda) transport — the parts that do NOT need the cohomology / flat-base-change engine. Leave any sorry that genuinely depends on FBC or full FGA representability documented and untouched, with a note on the blocker.

DELIVERABLE: the new subproject builds GREEN (lake build EXIT=0) with the FBC-free IdentityComponent sorries closed and axiom-clean; blueprint/DAG updated; it is positioned to merge back into AJC sorry-free like Albanese/GR. Reads from the AJC tree as source.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).
- **Subproject merge-back recipe (worked for T1 / Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso → AJC; reuse for Albanese / Cech back-merges).**

- **Wholesale lift beats cherry-pick** when the subproject built green on the same toolchain+mathlib rev: replace every diverged shared file with the subproject copy, add the new files, then repair AJC-only consumers. Verify first with a decl-name superset check (regex-extract `def|lemma|...` heads, `comm -23`) and a consumer grep for the decls that moved/disappeared.
- **Check for cross-module duplicate decl names BEFORE building**: two modules both imported by the root that declare the same fully-qualified name are a fatal clash. In T1, LBC's `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` had absorbed 13 decls of AJC's `PullbackTensorComp.lean` (incl. `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`) → the right fix was to **retire the superseded module** (its 8 unique helpers had no consumers).
- **Blueprint: port per-chapter, not per-project.** Wholesale-copy a chapter only after a comment-stripped cross-ref check: (refs of incoming chapter) ⊆ (its labels ∪ other AJC chapters' labels), and no other AJC chapter references a disappearing label. In T1 the TOS chapter ported cleanly; LBC's restructured `RelPicFunctor`/`LineBundlePullback` chapters did NOT (6 `\cref` sites in `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` would dangle) — kept AJC's copies. Their reconciliation is optional future work.
- Big-file build times on this box: `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (4592 L) + `TensorObjInverse.lean` (3424 L) + rest of the cone ≈ 15 min wall with warm mathlib cache.
- FBC bareBC Stage-2 patterns + scratch-iteration gotchas (session 0003-T2)

**Mate-factorization pattern (landed, reusable for other Beck-Chevalley mates):**

- `mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α |>.app c` factors as `adj₂.unit.app _ ≫ R₂.map (α.app _) ≫ R₂.map (H.map (adj₁.counit.app c))`. Proof: `simp [<def>, mateEquiv_apply]` then `erw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]; rfl` (three defeq-blocked `𝟙`s + one defeq head).
- Counit of `pullback q ⊣ pushforward q` at open immersion `q` is iso: `IsIso.of_isIso_fac_left (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit (restrictAdjunction q) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q) c)` with `haveI` on `(restrictAdjunction q).counit` iso via `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso q).hom)`.
- essImage → unit-iso: `Adjunction.isIso_unit_app_of_iso` (needs `pushforward` Full+Faithful — Mathlib instances for open immersions). Reverse `mem_essImage_of_unit_isIso`.
- **essImage of `p'_*` is cover-local** (`essImage_pushforward_of_openCover`, now in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`): site-level `restrictAdjunction` unit has plain `M.presheaf.map homOfLE` components; `isIso_iff_isIso_restrict` + `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` need NO push/pull coherence — components of the restricted map are again plain presheaf maps; match via `w''(O ⊓ w⁻¹R) = R ⊓ w''O` (`Set.image_inter_preimage`) + one `eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h)` factor. Poset-hom equalities: `congr 1` after `rw [← Functor.map_comp]` discharges by proof-irrelevance; op-level casts via `Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; Subsingleton.elim`.

**Scratch-iteration gotchas:**

- `lake env lean scratch.lean` does NOT apply lakefile `leanOptions`. Add `set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3` at scratch top or instance chains through functor categories fail mysteriously (e.g. `IsIso ((leftAdjointUniq ..).hom.app M)` composites). Destination files under `lake build` are fine.
- Mathlib-only lemmas iterate in ~3 min via a root-level scratch importing `Mathlib` (+ light project modules whose oleans exist) — 20x faster than the project file. Delete scratch before commit.
- `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1.lean` takes ~70+ min to compile (`maxHeartbeats 0`, documented in-file); it IS in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`'s import cone (via `CechTermAcyclic → CechSectionIdentificationBase → … `). Full verification of Unconditional edits costs hours if its olean is stale/missing.
- Beware RACING concurrent lake builds of the same project from leftover/parallel sessions (two `lean` processes on the same file observed) — olean corruption risk, duplicated 70-min work.
- Recipe: Stacks 00OE/00TT closed-point regularity for standard-smooth algebras (v4.31 Mathlib suffices)

**The smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula at maximal ideals needs NO transcendence-degree theory** — do not build Noether-normalisation dimension theory for it. Proven route (Albanese `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` + `CodimOneExtension.lean` §3.B, all axiom-clean):

- `ht M = #ι` for maximal `M ⊆ MvPolynomial ι k`: `Finite.induction_empty_option` (works with hypotheses depending on `ι` — the tactic auto-generalises `M`, `hM`); step = `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` (contraction maximal, `MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance exists for `Finite ι`) + `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`; transport via `RingEquiv.height_map/height_comap` + `Ideal.map/comap_isMaximal_of_equiv`.
- Lower bound `n ≤ ht m` in standard-smooth `S`: pull `m` back along `P.algebraMap_surjective` of the submersive presentation; `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le` (Krull height thm, relative form) + `spanFinrank(ker) ≤ #σ` via `P.span_range_relation_eq_ker`; quotient side identified with `m` via `RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk` + `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective`; cancel in ℕ∞ with `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`.

UniversallyOpen-to-Spec-field needs `haveI : Subsingleton ↥(Spec (.of k)) := inferInstanceAs (Subsingleton (PrimeSpectrum k))` (sometimes ALSO at the defeq-disguised type `↥(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left`).

But **morphism-level rw/assoc in the resulting goal FAILS**: `(Over.pullback φ).obj X` vs `pullback X.hom φ` index mismatch is opaque at instances transparency and `respectTransparency false` does NOT rescue rw or post-rw rfl. Working pattern: **point level** — calc chain of `rfl` steps redistributing `⇑(_ ≫ _).base` applications, `congrArg` for every rewrite (incl. `Subsingleton.elim`), `rw` only inside standalone well-typed subterms (e.g. `pullback.lift_fst` on an explicitly spelled lift, with the lift's w-proof given concretely — proof irrelevance makes the rfl steps match).

Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- Recipe: pole-divisor purity WITHOUT normality/UFD of regular local rings (Albanese `PolePurity.lean`, axiom-clean) — reuse for any "unmixedness of principal ideals" need.

Mathlib v4.31 has NO regular⟹UFD (Auslander–Buchsbaum), NO Serre normality criterion, NO Krull-domain $R=\bigcap_{\mathrm{ht}=1}R_\mathfrak p$; `RingTheory/RegularLocalRing/` is only `Defs.lean`. Do not build those. The elementary substitute chain:

- **Swap lemma** (`mem_span_singleton_of_swap_pair`): domain, $u\ne 0$, $v$ regular mod $(u)$, $ux, vx \in (b)$ ⟹ $x \in (b)$ via $b(ut-vs)=0$. Replaces the determinant trick / integral-closedness entirely.
- **Regular pair in regular local of dim ≥ 2** (`IsRegularLocalRing.exists_swap_pair_of_two_le_ringKrullDim`): $u \in \mathfrak m\setminus\mathfrak m^2$ + project lemmas `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` and `isDomain_of_regularLocal` (00NP, AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean — CONFIRMED axiom-clean); pick $v$ lifting a nonzero element of the quotient's maximal ideal.
- **Ass-prime height bound** (`Ideal.height_le_one_of_colon_span_singleton`): prime $Q=((b):y)$ with $A_Q$ regular ⟹ $\mathrm{ht}\,Q\le 1$; the colon persists to $\mathfrak m_{A_Q}$ (map ≤ colon via `Localization.AtPrime.map_eq_maximalIdeal`, colon proper ⟹ ≤ maximalIdeal).
- **Main thm over $R_\mathfrak p$** so the associated prime lands below $\mathfrak p$ (`exists_le_isAssociatedPrime_of_isNoetherianRing` + `isAssociatedPrime_iff`); transfer with the golden Mathlib trio: `IsLocalization.height_under` (heights under comap of localization), `IsLocalization.localizationLocalizationAtPrimeIsoLocalization` (double localization ≃ₐ), `IsRegularLocalRing.of_ringEquiv`.
- **Scheme wrapper traps**: state membership as `algebraMap (X.presheaf.stalk z) X.functionField).range` (instances `stalkFunctionFieldAlgebra`, `functionField_isScalarTower`, `functionField_isFractionRing_of_isAffineOpen` are all ready-made for integral X); point-from-prime via `hU.fromSpec` + `hU.isLocalization_stalk'` (takes the PrimeSpectrum point directly — no primeIdealOf round-trip); `Order.coheight z = 1` via CoheightBridge `ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` + `IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`. Hypotheses of form `∀ q, IsRegularLocalRing (Localization.AtPrime q)` MUST use instance binder `∀ (q : Ideal R) [q.IsPrime], …` (plain `→` fails elaboration). `Submodule.mem_colon_singleton` gives `r • y ∈ …` — always explicit `rw [smul_eq_mul]`, never `simpa [smul_eq_mul] using` (fails). `IsLocalization.mk'_surjective` returns a beta-unreduced match — fix with `replace hcs : IsLocalization.mk' … = g := hcs`. Quotient-module annihilator bridge: `Algebra.smul_def` + `Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq` (NOT `map_smul`/`Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul`).

Remaining Milne 3.3 gaps after this: substeps 1–3 (difference rational map + slicing + $\Phi^*\mathcal O_{G,e}$; needs function-field pullback for `Scheme.RationalMap`) and 4b (diagonal codim bound: `ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_add_card` in Mathlib `KrullDimension/Regular.lean` is the Krull tool; diagonal ideal $n$-generation from smoothness still to build). For non-closed diagonal points, the slicing in substep 2 works at the GENERIC point of the fiber $\mathrm{pr}_1^{-1}(x) = \operatorname{Spec}\kappa(x)\times_{\bar k}X$ (irreducible by geometric irreducibility; its generic point maps to $\eta_X \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)$ under $\mathrm{pr}_2$) — this resolves the "Milne argues with closed points" worry.
- **AJC build-wall reality (run 0003/0007): `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1` costs HOURS under load, and racing builds are endemic.**

- `LegMid1` ("~70 min" documented) took **5h40m** wall at 100% CPU with box load ~65; its rebuild also cascades: `LegMid2 → LegTop → LegAux → CechSectionIdentification → CechAugmentedResolution → CechTermAcyclic → CechToHigherDirectImage` all rebuild (each 20 min–2h+ under load). Budget ~8-12h wall for any change that invalidates the `CechSectionIdentification*` chain; NEVER let its oleans go missing.
- Before ANY `lake build` in AJC: `pgrep -f 'bin/lean'` + check for other sessions' `lake build` (cwd via `/proc/PID/cwd`). This run had FOUR overlapping builders (two orphaned leans from killed lakes, my rebuild, another session's full build). Orphaned leans (parent lake dead) waste their work — lake re-runs the job anyway; kill them.
- A long-running lean of mine was SIGTERM-killed at 3h39m by an external actor (likely another session's cleanup) — detached builds are not safe from concurrent sessions.
- `lake env lean` on a file whose import cone has a MISSING olean fails immediately ('object file does not exist') — iterate by temporarily commenting the blocked import and stubbing the 1-2 facts it supplies, then restore for the final build (worked cleanly for `AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`).
- Instance-synthesis quirk: in big files, `IsOpenImmersion (pullback.fst g' U.ι)` and even `IsOpenImmersion (Opens.ι ...)` can FAIL to synthesize inside composite `exact` terms (metavariable/universe timing) though global instances exist — bind them first with `haveI h := inferInstance` (or a lemma) and pass via `@`-application.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, and `## Next`; add or rename sections if another shape is clearer. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this Horizon session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

8	/-!
9	# FGA representability of the Picard scheme (A.2.c)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.c** assembly file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
12	project's positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It wires the
13	Quot-scheme engine (A.2.b, sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`) and the
14	étale-sheafified relative Picard functor (A.1.c, sibling
15	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`) into the FGA representability statement for
16	`Pic_{C/k}`, following Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (cf. FGA Explained
17	Ch. 9 §9.4; arXiv:math/0504020).
18	
19	## Status (iter-196 carrier-soundness probe)
20	
21	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive
22	type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
23	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex`). The carrier
24	sorry bodies have been isolated to `Prop`-valued typeclass instance constructors
25	(`⟨sorry⟩`) following the iter-196 carrier-soundness probe (Option A
26	`Functor.IsRepresentable` pattern). The carrier definitions themselves are now
27	`noncomputable` extractions via `Classical.choose` / `Classical.choice` from the
28	typeclass existence fields, so consumers that *quantify* over the typeclasses
29	(rather than use the global instances) are kernel-clean.
30	
31	The 6 pinned declarations are:
32	
33	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **Picard scheme**
34	   `Pic_{C/k}` as an object of `Over (Spec k)`, the witness extracted from
35	   the `Functor.RepresentableBy` produced by `representable`.
36	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.abelMap` (def, ~6 LOC) — the
37	   **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` as a natural
38	   transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`.
39	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
40	   — the Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma** (Kleiman §4
41	   Lem. `lm:qt`) packaging the descent from a flat-and-proper equivalence
42	   relation `R ⊆ Z ×_k Z` on a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Z` to a
43	   representing quotient `Q`.
44	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
45	   the **FGA representability theorem** (Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`,
46	   specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k` and `X = C` a smooth
47	   proper geometrically integral curve): the étale-sheafified relative
48	   Picard functor is represented by `PicScheme`.
49	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.groupSchemeStructure` (instance,
50	   ~6 LOC) — the **group-scheme structure** on `PicScheme`: a `GrpObj`
51	   instance obtained by Yoneda transport of the abelian-group structure
52	   on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
53	
54	## Note on type expressivity
55	
56	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
57	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
58	
59	- `PicScheme C : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a scheme over `k`, not a
60	  tautological `Spec k`.
61	- `abelMap C : divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C` — a genuine natural
62	  transformation of presheaves, not `𝟙 _` (it sends a relative effective
63	  divisor to its associated invertible sheaf).
64	- `smoothProperQuotient` carries the four Kleiman §4 Lem. `lm:qt`
65	  hypotheses (Z represented, R representable, smooth+proper first
66	  projection, étale surjection) and concludes `P.IsRepresentable`. The
67	  hypotheses are not vacuous: any one of (i)–(iv) can fail for a
68	  non-quotient morphism of presheaves.
69	- `representable C : (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C)` — the
70	  substantive Yoneda-bijection structure between `Hom(T, PicScheme C)` and
71	  `picSharp C |_T`.
72	- `groupSchemeStructure C : GrpObj (PicScheme C)` — a genuine group-object
73	  structure, with multiplication / inverse / unit morphisms satisfying the
74	  group axioms.
75	
76	The two file-internal auxiliary defs `picSharp` and `divFunctor` (placeholders
77	for the sibling-chapter functors `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` and `Div_{C/k}`) are
78	carrier defs extracted from `Prop`-valued typeclasses `HasPicSharp` and
79	`HasDivFunctor` using `Classical.choice`. They ship in the
80	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` namespace, marked with docstrings
81	explaining they will be re-exports of the sibling-chapter landing once
82	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` and `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` land (forward refs
83	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp` and `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.divFunctor`
84	per their blueprint `\lean{...}` pins). Their bodies collapse to a one-line
85	`:= ...` re-export at that point.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` (640 LOC,
90	5 pins). Source: Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (FGA Explained Ch. 9 §9.4;
91	arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 25–35), specifically:
92	- §3 Def. `dfn:Abel` (the Abel map) + Thm. `th:repDiv` (relative effective
93	  divisors representable by an open subscheme of the Hilbert scheme),
94	- §4 Main Thm. `th:main` + Cor. `cor:algsch` (FGA representability),
95	- §4 Lem. `lm:qt` (smooth-proper quotient of étale sheaves).
96	-/

110	/-! ## §0. File-internal placeholders for the sibling-chapter functors
111	
112	The two presheaves `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` (the étale-sheafified relative Picard
113	functor; sibling chapter `Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`, A.1.c, pin
114	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp`) and `Div_{C/k}` (the relative-effective-
115	divisor functor; sibling chapter `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`, A.2.b, pin
116	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.divFunctor`) are forward references; the sibling
117	Lean files have not yet landed. For the iter-176 file-skeleton we encode
118	both as carrier defs extracted from `Prop`-valued typeclasses in this namespace
119	so the 5 pinned declarations below can mention them substantively.
120

122	
123	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor
124	`Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`. The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique

127	class HasPicSharp {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) : Prop where
128	  has_pic_sharp : Nonempty ((Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u)
129

131	functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét} : (Sch/k)^op ⥤ Type u` of
132	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` (A.1.c).
133

135	name `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp`; once that sibling file lands the
136	present def becomes a one-line re-export
137	`fun C => AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp C`. Until then it is extracted
138	from `HasPicSharp.has_pic_sharp` via `Classical.choice`. -/
139	noncomputable def picSharp {k : Type u} [Field k]
140	    (_C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [HasPicSharp _C] :
141	    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
142	  Classical.choice (HasPicSharp.has_pic_sharp (C := _C))
143	
144	/-- Existence instance for `HasPicSharp`. This is the single sorry-carrying site
145	for `picSharp`; the sorry is isolated here rather than baked into `picSharp`'s
146	body. -/
147	noncomputable instance instHasPicSharp {k : Type u} [Field k]
148	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) : HasPicSharp C :=
149	  ⟨sorry⟩
150	
151	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of the relative-divisor functor `Div_{C/k}`.
152	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for this
153	carrier. -/
154	class HasDivFunctor {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) : Prop where
155	  has_div_functor : Nonempty ((Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u)
156	
157	/-- **File-internal placeholder** for the relative-divisor functor
158	`Div_{C/k} : (Sch/k)^op ⥤ Type u`, sending a `k`-scheme `T` to the set of
159	relative effective Cartier divisors on `C ×_k T` flat over `T`.
160	
161	The blueprint chapter `Picard_QuotScheme.tex` pins the Quot/Hilbert engine
162	under `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme`; the divisor functor here is the
163	open sub-functor cut out by the effective-divisor criterion (Kleiman §3
164	Thm. `th:repDiv`). Once `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` lands the present def
165	collapses to a one-line re-export. Until then it is extracted from
166	`HasDivFunctor.has_div_functor` via `Classical.choice`. -/
167	noncomputable def divFunctor {k : Type u} [Field k]
168	    (_C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [HasDivFunctor _C] :
169	    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
170	  Classical.choice (HasDivFunctor.has_div_functor (C := _C))
171	
172	/-- Existence instance for `HasDivFunctor`. This is the single sorry-carrying site
173	for `divFunctor`. -/

176	  ⟨sorry⟩

178	end PicScheme
179	
180	/-! ## §1. The Picard scheme
181	
182	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
183	`C/k` is the `k`-scheme representing the étale-sheafified relative Picard
184	functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`. It is supplied by Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`
185	(specialised to `S = Spec k`, `X = C` via Cor. `cor:algsch`); the
186	representability theorem itself is the next declaration (`representable`),
187	and `PicScheme` is the witness scheme extracted from it.
188	
189	The scheme is separated, locally of finite type over `k`, and a disjoint
190	union of open quasi-projective `k`-subschemes; the abelian-group-scheme
191	structure is the content of `groupSchemeStructure`.
192	
193	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_scheme` (Kleiman §4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/
194	
195	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of a scheme over `Spec k` that represents
196	`picSharp C`. The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying
197	site for `PicScheme`; consumers that quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` as a
198	hypothesis remain kernel-clean. -/
199	class HasPicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
200	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
201	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
202	  has_pic_scheme : ∃ (X : Over (Spec (.of k))),
203	    Nonempty ((PicScheme.picSharp C).RepresentableBy X)
204	
205	/-- The **Picard scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically
206	integral curve `C/k`.
207	
208	Encoded as an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))` (a `k`-scheme), carrying the
209	substantive identity "this is the scheme representing `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`"
210	via the `RepresentableBy` witness produced by
211	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable`. The structural
212	properties (separated, locally of finite type, disjoint union of open
213	quasi-projectives) live on the underlying scheme via the standard
214	instances of `Over (Spec (.of k))`.
215	
216	iter-177+: the body extracts the representing object from
217	`representable C`, e.g.
218	`(representable C).choose` modulo a `Functor.RepresentableBy` witness; or
219	constructs it directly via the four-step Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` proof
220	(Hilbert-polynomial stratification, `m`-regularity bound, Abel-map
221	factorisation, smooth-proper quotient). The body is extracted from
222	`HasPicScheme.has_pic_scheme` via `Classical.choose`. -/
223	noncomputable def PicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
224	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
225	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
226	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasPicScheme C] :
227	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
228	  (HasPicScheme.has_pic_scheme (C := C)).choose
229	
230	/-- Existence instance for `HasPicScheme`. This is the single sorry-carrying site
231	for `PicScheme`. -/
232	noncomputable instance instHasPicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
233	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
234	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
235	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : HasPicScheme C :=
236	  ⟨sorry⟩
237	
238	namespace PicScheme
239	
240	/-! ## §2. The Abel map — line-bundle / Quot correspondence
241	
242	The bridge from line bundles on `C ×_k T` to the Quot scheme is the **Abel
243	map**: a relative effective divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` (an `S`-flat
244	closed subscheme whose ideal is invertible) determines a `T`-point of the
245	Hilbert functor `Hilb_{C/k}(T)` (hence of `Div_{C/k}(T)`), and the dual
246	ideal sheaf `O_{C ×_k T}(D) := I_D⁻¹` is an invertible sheaf on the
247	relative curve, representing a class in `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)`.
248	
249	The assignment `D ↦ [O_{C ×_k T}(D)]` is functorial in `T` (pullback of an
250	ideal sheaf along `id_C ×_k g : C ×_k T' ⟶ C ×_k T` takes the ideal of `D`
251	to the ideal of `D_{T'}`, since `D` is flat over `T` and hence
252	`D_{T'} = D ×_T T'` is again a relative effective divisor by Kleiman §3
253	Lem. `lm:ctn`).
254	
255	Blueprint reference: `lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence` (Kleiman §3
256	Def. `dfn:Abel` + Thm. `th:repDiv`). -/
257	
258	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of the Abel map `Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
259	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
260	`abelMap`. -/
261	class HasAbelMap {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
262	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
263	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
264	  has_abel_map : Nonempty (divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C)
265	
266	/-- The **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`, sending a
267	relative effective Cartier divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` to its associated
268	invertible sheaf `[O_{C ×_k T}(D)] = [I_D⁻¹]`.
269	
270	A natural transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`. The source
271	`divFunctor C` is the relative-divisor functor (the open subfunctor of the
272	Hilbert/Quot scheme cut out by the effective-divisor criterion); the target
273	`picSharp C` is the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor.
274	
275	iter-177+: the body constructs the natural transformation component-wise:
276	for each test `T`, send a relative effective Cartier divisor `D` on
277	`C ×_k T` to the dual of its ideal sheaf, then take the étale-sheafified
278	class. Naturality is the functoriality of the dual-of-ideal-sheaf
279	construction under pullback (Stacks 01HG + Kleiman §3 Lem. `lm:ctn`).
280	The body is extracted from `HasAbelMap.has_abel_map` via `Classical.choice`. -/
281	noncomputable def abelMap {k : Type u} [Field k]
282	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
283	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
284	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasAbelMap C] :
285	    divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C :=
286	  Classical.choice (HasAbelMap.has_abel_map (C := C))
287	
288	/-- Existence instance for `HasAbelMap`. This is the single sorry-carrying site
289	for `abelMap`. -/
290	noncomputable instance instHasAbelMap {k : Type u} [Field k]
291	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
292	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
293	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : HasAbelMap C :=
294	  ⟨sorry⟩
295	
296	/-! ## §3. The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent
297	
298	The structural lemma underlying Step 4 of Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` is the
299	Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma**: given a surjection
300	`α : Z ⟶ P` of étale sheaves with `R := Z ×_P Z` representable, `Z`
301	quasi-projective, and the first projection `R ⟶ Z` smooth and proper,
302	the quotient `P` is representable by a quasi-projective scheme.
303	
304	Kleiman's proof:
305	1. `R ↪ Z ×_k Z` is a flat (from (iv)) and proper monomorphism, hence a
306	   closed immersion by EGA IV 8.11.5.
307	2. By the theory of flat-and-proper effective equivalence relations on
308	   quasi-projective schemes (Altman–Kleiman), there exist a
309	   quasi-projective scheme `Q` and a faithfully flat, projective map
310	   `Z ⟶ Q` with `R = Z ×_Q Z`. The map `Z ⟶ Q` is smooth (its fibers
311	   coincide with those of `R ⟶ Z`, which are smooth).
312	3. In the category of étale sheaves, both `Q` and `P` are coequalisers of
313	   `R ⇒ Z`; uniqueness of the coequaliser gives `Q ≃ P`.
314	
315	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_proper_quotient` (Kleiman §4 Lem.
316	`lm:qt`). -/
317	
318	/-- Typeclass asserting the conclusion of the Altman–Kleiman smooth-proper
319	quotient lemma: that the target presheaf `P` of an étale-sheaf surjection
320	satisfying the smooth-proper hypotheses is representable.
321	
322	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
323	`smoothProperQuotient`; per the iter-196 carrier-soundness probe (verdict
324	CONFIRMED iter-199), the theorem body itself is axiom-clean (extracts via
325	this typeclass field), and consumers that quantify over
326	`[HasSmoothProperQuotient α]` as a hypothesis remain kernel-clean.
327	
328	The mathematical witness is the Altman–Kleiman effective-equivalence-
329	relation theorem: a flat-and-proper equivalence relation `R ↪ Y ×_k Y`
330	on a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Y` admits a quasi-projective quotient
331	`Q` with `Y ⟶ Q` faithfully flat and projective; combined with EGA IV
332	8.11.5 (a proper monomorphism is a closed immersion) and the coequaliser-
333	uniqueness in the category of étale sheaves, `Q` represents `P`. Both
334	inputs (Altman–Kleiman descent and EGA IV 8.11.5 as a named bridge) are
335	missing from Mathlib at the project's pinned revision; the closure is
336	gated on those Mathlib gaps per the blueprint chapter
337	§"Sorry 4 — smoothProperQuotient body". -/
338	class HasSmoothProperQuotient {k : Type u} [Field k]
339	    {Z P : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u}
340	    (_α : Z ⟶ P) : Prop where
341	  is_representable : P.IsRepresentable
342	
343	/-- Existence instance for `HasSmoothProperQuotient`. This is the single
344	sorry-carrying site for `smoothProperQuotient`; the `⟨sorry⟩` is isolated
345	here rather than baked into the theorem body. -/
346	instance instHasSmoothProperQuotient {k : Type u} [Field k]
347	    {Z P : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u}
348	    (α : Z ⟶ P) : HasSmoothProperQuotient α :=
349	  ⟨sorry⟩
350	
351	/-- **The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent of an
352	étale-sheaf surjection.**
353	
354	Let `Z, P : (Sch/k)^op ⥤ Type u` be presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`, and let
355	`α : Z ⟶ P` be a natural transformation. Assume:
356	1. `Z` is representable by a `k`-scheme `Y` (encoded by the
357	   `Z.RepresentableBy Y` hypothesis),
358	2. the pullback presheaf `R := Z ×_P Z` is representable by a `k`-scheme `R`
359	   (encoded by the `(Limits.pullback α α).RepresentableBy R` hypothesis),
360	3. the first projection `π : R ⟶ Y` (after representation) is smooth and
361	   proper (encoded by the typeclass hypotheses `[Smooth π.left]` and
362	   `[IsProper π.left]`),
363	4. `α` is an étale surjection (encoded as the lift property: every
364	   `T`-point `p ∈ P(T)` lifts to a `T'`-point `z ∈ Z(T')` for some test
365	   morphism `T ⟶ T'`).
366	
367	Then `P` is representable (by a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Q`).
368	
369	iter-199: the body extracts the conclusion from the `HasSmoothProperQuotient`
370	typeclass, following the iter-196 carrier-soundness probe pattern (verdict
371	CONFIRMED iter-199). The mathematical content — the Altman–Kleiman
372	effective-equivalence-relation descent + EGA IV 8.11.5 (proper
373	monomorphism is a closed immersion) — lives in
374	`instHasSmoothProperQuotient` as the single sorry-carrying instance.
375	Consumers that quantify over `[HasSmoothProperQuotient α]` as a hypothesis
376	(rather than using the global instance) are kernel-clean. -/
377	theorem smoothProperQuotient {k : Type u} [Field k]
378	    {Z P : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u}
379	    (α : Z ⟶ P)
380	    (Y : Over (Spec (.of k)))
381	    (_hZ : Z.RepresentableBy Y)
382	    (R : Over (Spec (.of k)))
383	    (_hR : (Limits.pullback α α).RepresentableBy R)
384	    (π : R ⟶ Y)
385	    [Smooth π.left] [IsProper π.left]
386	    (_hα : ∀ ⦃T : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ⦄ (p : P.obj T),
387	        ∃ (T' : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ) (e : T ⟶ T') (z : Z.obj T'),
388	          α.app T' z = P.map e p)
389	    [HasSmoothProperQuotient α] :
390	    P.IsRepresentable :=
391	  HasSmoothProperQuotient.is_representable (_α := α)
392	
393	/-! ## §4. The FGA representability theorem
394	
395	Grothendieck's existence theorem for the Picard scheme (Kleiman §4 Thm.
396	`th:main`), specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k`,
397	`X = C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve: the étale-sheafified
398	relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` is representable by a `k`-scheme,
399	separated and locally of finite type, which is a disjoint union of open
400	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes. The representing scheme is the **Picard
401	scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of `PicScheme` above.
402	
403	Blueprint reference: `thm:fga_pic_representability` (Kleiman §4 Thm.
404	`th:main` + Cor. `cor:algsch`). -/
405	
406	/-- Typeclass asserting that `picSharp C` is representable by `PicScheme C`.
407	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
408	`representable`. -/
409	class PicSharpRepresentable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
410	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
411	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
412	  has_representable : Nonempty ((picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C))
413	
414	/-- **FGA representability of the Picard scheme.**
415	
416	Let `k` be a field and `C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
417	over `k`. Then the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`
418	is representable by the Picard scheme `PicScheme C`.
419	
420	iter-177+: the body assembles the four-step Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` proof:
421	1. Stratify `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` by Hilbert polynomial into a disjoint open
422	   cover `(P^φ)_φ`.
423	2. For each `φ`, bound twists by `m`-regularity to reduce to `P^{φ_0}_0`.
424	3. Factor through the Abel map `A_{C/k}` of `abelMap` to obtain
425	   `Z := P^{φ_0}_0 ×_{Pic^♯} Div_{C/k}`, a quasi-projective open of
426	   `Div_{C/k}`.
427	4. Apply `smoothProperQuotient` to the projection `Z ⟶ P^{φ_0}_0` to
428	   represent each `P^{φ_0}_0`; reassemble over `φ` for the full
429	   `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
430	
431	The body is extracted from `PicSharpRepresentable.has_representable` via
432	`Classical.choice`. -/
433	noncomputable def representable {k : Type u} [Field k]
434	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
435	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
436	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [PicSharpRepresentable C] :
437	    (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C) :=
438	  Classical.choice (PicSharpRepresentable.has_representable (C := C))
439	
440	/-- Existence instance for `PicSharpRepresentable`. This is the single
441	sorry-carrying site for `representable`. -/
442	noncomputable instance instPicSharpRepresentable {k : Type u} [Field k]
443	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
444	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
445	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : PicSharpRepresentable C :=
446	  ⟨sorry⟩
447	
448	/-! ## §5. The abelian group-scheme structure
449	
450	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` inherits an abelian group-scheme structure
451	from the tensor product of invertible sheaves on `C ×_k T`. The relative
452	Picard presheaf `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)` is already an abelian
453	group at every `T` (the quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup),
454	functorially in `T`; this lifts to the étale sheafification (sheafification
455	of an abelian-presheaf is an abelian-sheaf, since it commutes with finite
456	products in the value category), and then to a group-scheme structure on
457	the representing scheme by Yoneda.
458	
459	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_is_group_scheme` (Kleiman §2 Def. `df:Pfs` +
460	§4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/
461	
462	/-- Typeclass asserting that `PicScheme C` carries a `GrpObj` structure.
463	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
464	`groupSchemeStructure`. -/
465	class PicSchemeGroupObject {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
466	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
467	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
468	  has_group_object : Nonempty (GrpObj (PicScheme C))
469	
470	/-- **The Picard scheme is a `k`-group scheme.**
471	
472	The Picard scheme `PicScheme C` carries a canonical `GrpObj` structure (a
473	group-object structure in `Over (Spec (.of k))`), obtained by Yoneda
474	transport of the abelian-group structure on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)` (sum:
475	`[L] + [M] = [L ⊗ M]`; inverse: `-[L] = [L⁻¹]`; unit: `[O_{C ×_k T}]`).
476	
477	The full abelian-group-scheme refinement is the conjunction of this `GrpObj`
478	instance with the `IsCommMonObj (PicScheme C)` predicate (commutativity of
479	the multiplication), which holds because tensor product of invertible
480	sheaves is symmetric; `IsCommMonObj` is a separate instance threaded on top
481	of `GrpObj` in iter-177+.
482	
483	iter-177+: the body invokes the Yoneda lemma to transport the natural
484	abelian-group structure on `T ↦ picSharp C |_T` to the multiplication /
485	inverse / unit morphisms on `PicScheme C`; the group axioms hold at the
486	representable level functorially in `T`, hence on the representing scheme
487	by Yoneda. The body is extracted from `PicSchemeGroupObject.has_group_object`
488	via `Classical.choice`. -/
489	noncomputable instance groupSchemeStructure {k : Type u} [Field k]
490	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
491	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
492	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [PicSchemeGroupObject C] :
493	    GrpObj (PicScheme C) :=
494	  Classical.choice (PicSchemeGroupObject.has_group_object (C := C))
495	
496	/-- Existence instance for `PicSchemeGroupObject`. This is the single
497	sorry-carrying site for `groupSchemeStructure`. -/
498	noncomputable instance instPicSchemeGroupObject {k : Type u} [Field k]
499	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
500	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
501	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : PicSchemeGroupObject C :=
502	  ⟨sorry⟩
503	
504	end PicScheme
505	
506	end Scheme
507	
508	end AlgebraicGeometry

967	functorial actions of this chapter). -/

969	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T)

1096

1098	Zariski sheaf (Kleiman §2 L1292–L1302). To obtain a representable
1099	functor in the sense of `chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability`

1101	étale sheaf** `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét} := (PicSharp_{C/k})^{∼ét}`. Kleiman §4
1102	(Theorem `th:main`) represents precisely this sheafified functor.

1105	`presheafToSheaf J _` applied to the bundled `PicSharp.presheaf` for a
1106	**parameter** Grothendieck topology `J` on `Over (Spec k)` — because

1110	canonical étale topology once it lands in Mathlib.

1126	Picard functor**

1164

1166	abelian-group-valued: the forgetful functor `AddCommGrpCat ⥤ Type`

/-! ## §2. The identity component of the Picard scheme

We specialise the abstract identity-component substrate to
`G = PicScheme C`, the Picard scheme of a smooth proper geometrically
integral curve `C/k` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`).

Blueprint reference: `def:pic_zero_subscheme` (Kleiman §5 opening + Prp.
`prp:pic0`). -/

namespace Scheme

/-- The **identity component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.

Defined as the identity component
`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` of the Picard scheme
`Pic_{C/k}` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`,
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`). By
`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`, `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an
open and closed subgroup scheme of `Pic_{C/k}` of finite type over `k`,
geometrically irreducible, and its formation commutes with extension of the
base field.

iter-186+: the body unwinds to `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)`
once the `[LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom]` instance lands on
`PicScheme C` (it follows from the Kleiman §4 structure: `Pic_{C/k}` is
locally of finite type as the disjoint union of open quasi-projective
`k`-subschemes). For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/

    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
  sorry

/-! ## §3. The degree map

The disjoint-union structure of `Pic_{C/k}` (a disjoint union of open
quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, indexed by Hilbert polynomial via
`PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient`) stratifies its `T`-points by the leading
coefficient of the Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf
relative to a fixed degree-one polarisation. On `k`-points this gives the
**degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.

Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree_pic` (Milne III.1, p.~88). -/

namespace PicScheme

/-- The **degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.

Sends a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` --- a morphism
`Spec k ⟶ (PicScheme C).left` --- to the leading coefficient of the
Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf `L` on `C` (relative
to a fixed degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`). By Riemann--Roch,
`χ(C, L ⊗ O_C(n)) = n · deg L + 1 - g`, so the degree is the leading
coefficient of `Φ_L(n)`, well-defined on the isomorphism class `[L]` and on
the `k`-point `λ` (because `PicScheme C` represents the étale-sheafified
relative Picard functor).

The degree map is a group homomorphism for the additive structure on
`Pic_{C/k}(k)` (tensor product on `L`) and the standard `(ℤ, +)`. The full
group-homomorphism refinement / functoriality in `k` lives as a follow-up
lemma in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the underlying function.

iter-186+: the body extracts the representing invertible sheaf from
`(PicScheme.representable C)`, forms its Hilbert polynomial via the project's
Hilbert-polynomial machinery (sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`), and returns
the leading coefficient. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
`sorry`. -/
noncomputable def degree {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
    (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ :=
  sorry

end PicScheme

/-! ## §4. `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety

The terminal statement of the chapter identifies `Pic⁰_{C/k}` with an
abelian variety of dimension `g(C)` --- the Jacobian variety of `C`. In
this project, "abelian variety" is the conjunction of the four properties
`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`
threaded through `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` and consumed by
`AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AlbaneseUP`.

Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety` (Kleiman §5
Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`; cf. Milne §I.1, Rmk. III.1.4(e)). -/

namespace Pic0Scheme

/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety.**

For `C/k` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus `g = g(C)`
over a field `k`, the identity component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` of the Picard scheme
is an *abelian variety over* `k`: a smooth, proper, geometrically
irreducible `k`-group scheme. (Commutativity follows automatically from
Milne §I.1, Cor. 1.4, and is not separately stated here.)

The bundled statement: the four pieces `[IsProper] ∧ [Smooth] ∧
[GeometricallyIrreducible] ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)` hold on
`(Pic0Scheme C).hom` and `(Pic0Scheme C)` respectively.

Dimension `g = g(C)`: not stated separately at the file-skeleton level
(it requires `dim_k (Pic0Scheme C).left = AlgebraicGeometry.genus C`, which
involves the Krull-dimension API on the underlying scheme); iter-186+
follow-up.

iter-186+: the body assembles the four conjuncts from
- `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (clopen subscheme of
  `PicScheme C`),
- Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (`X/k` projective + geom. integral ⟹
  `Pic⁰_{X/k}` quasi-projective; upgrade to projective when geom. normal,
  which holds for smooth proper curves of positive genus),
- Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Ex.~`ex:jac` (smoothness of `Pic_{X/k}` at the
  identity for smooth proper curves; hence smooth of dimension
  `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)` everywhere by uniform smoothness on the open
  identity component).
For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
  sorry

/-- **Dimension of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals the genus of `C`.**

Milne~§III.1, Rmk.~1.4(e): "The dimension of `J` is the genus of `C`".
For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k` of genus
`g = g(C)`, the topological Krull dimension of the underlying scheme of
`Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals `g(C)`. By Kleiman~§5 Cor.~`cor:sm`, the inequality
`dim Pic_{C/k} ≤ dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` is always an equality at points where
`Pic_{C/k}` is smooth, and for smooth proper curves
(`SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom`) the identity component is smooth
(Kleiman~§5 Ex.~`ex:jac`), so the dimension equals `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)`
by `def:genus`. -/
theorem finrank_eq_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
    topologicalKrullDim (Pic0Scheme C).left = (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : WithBot ℕ∞) :=
  sorry

/-- **`k`-points of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` are the kernel of the degree map.**

Milne~§III.1, p.~88: `Pic⁰(C)` is the group of isomorphism classes of
invertible sheaves of degree zero on `C`. For a smooth proper geometrically
integral curve `C/k`, a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` lies in the image of
the inclusion `Pic⁰_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}` (the inclusion of
`def:pic_zero_subscheme`, packaged here via the existence of the
inclusion morphism) if and only if `degree C λ = 0`.

The statement-level pin packages two pieces: existence of the inclusion
morphism `Pic⁰_{C/k} ⟶ Pic_{C/k}` (extracted from
`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` once `PicScheme C` has the
`GrpObj` + `LocallyOfFiniteType` instances), together with the
characterisation of `k`-points factoring through it as those with degree
zero. -/
theorem kPoints_iff_kerDegree {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
    Nonempty (Σ' (inc : (Pic0Scheme C).left ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
      ∀ (lambda : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
        (∃ mu : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left, mu ≫ inc = lambda) ↔
          PicScheme.degree C lambda = 0) :=
  sorry

end Pic0Scheme

end Scheme

end AlgebraicGeometry
